behaviors.description: Traveling-salesman heuristic route length is <=1.5x optimal for up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm quality itself (pure computation). No OS/network/subprocess dependency. A mock is not needed and would not improve detection; key risk is algorithm regression against an exact optimal baseline.
behaviors.test_description: Generate deterministic coordinate sets (n<=20), compute heuristic route length, compute optimal route length with an exact in-process solver (e.g., Held-Karp/branch-and-bound for small n), and assert heuristic/optimal <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: Delivery time windows are correctly computed from stop-to-stop distance and configured road-type average speed.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is module wiring: route segmenting + distance provider + road-type speed config + time accumulation. A pure unit test on one function can miss mapping/config integration errors.
behaviors.test_description: Run the estimator through its public service boundary with a multi-segment route containing different road types, using real config loading and real distance module (or fixed coordinates that produce known distances). Assert per-leg and cumulative ETA/time windows match expected math.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift time and returns the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is deterministic arithmetic/threshold logic in the validator. No external infra is required to catch true logic bugs in reject/accept and excess calculation.
behaviors.test_description: Call validator directly with representative totals and remaining-shift values (below, equal, above threshold), and assert rejection flag plus exact excess duration (including rounding/units rules).

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (no results), service skips it, optimizes remaining stops, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real HTTP/API contract handling. In-process mocks can hide real failures (status/payload shape/null fields/timeouts). Real HTTP interaction is the failure boundary here.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end service call against a controllable geocoding test endpoint over real HTTP where one address returns an explicit 'no results' payload and others return valid coordinates. Assert output route excludes failed address, optimization still runs on remaining stops, and skipped-address report is accurate.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses are consolidated into one stop with combined delivery items and are not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Need to validate cross-module behavior: input normalization/dedup + item merge + optimizer input/output. A single-function unit test may miss that deduped stops still get duplicated later in pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke route-planning pipeline with repeated addresses carrying distinct items, then assert exactly one stop per unique address enters/appears in final route and merged item counts are preserved.
